highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.179 Released
highlights.summary: This release fixes mid-stream connection drops so partial responses are preserved instead of showing raw errors, and the spinner no longer gets stuck on 'running tool'. It also fixes mouse-wheel scrolling in WSL2 under Windows Terminal and VS Code, which had regressed in v2.1.172. If you use Claude Code on Windows with WSL2, this update is worth grabbing immediately.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.178 Released
highlights.summary: This version introduces a powerful new `Tool(param:value)` syntax for permission rules, letting you match tools by their input parameters — for example, `Agent(model:opus)` will block subagents from using the Opus model. It also improves Skills loading so nested `.claude/skills` directories are picked up when you're working in subdirectories, with sensible name-clash resolution. These are significant workflow improvements for teams managing multi-agent setups.

highlights.title: Anthropic Pauses Credit Change for Claude Code
highlights.summary: Anthropic had announced that the Claude Agent SDK, `claude -p`, and third-party apps built on the Agent SDK would move from subscription rate limits to a dedicated monthly credit system, but has now paused that change. If you rely on programmatic Claude Code usage via the Agent SDK, your current billing behavior stays the same for now. Keep an eye on official communications for when this change is rescheduled.

highlights.title: Show HN: Claude Code for Visual Studio (Native Diff with Accept/Reject)
highlights.summary: A community developer built Claude Code integration for Microsoft Visual Studio (not VS Code), filling a gap since only VS Code and JetBrains have official plugins. It uses the same protocol as the official integrations, so you get native diff views with accept/reject buttons just like in VS Code. If you work in Visual Studio for .NET or C++ projects, this is worth trying out.

highlights.title: Running Claude Code Offline on an M3 Pro with Qwen3.6
highlights.summary: This guide walks through setting up Claude Code in an air-gapped environment on an Apple M3 Pro using the Qwen3.6 local model, which is useful for security-sensitive or offline workflows. It's part of a broader SRE handbook for Claude Code, covering how to configure the tool to point at a local model endpoint instead of Anthropic's API. Developers with strict data-residency or network restrictions will find this particularly valuable.

highlights.title: From Zero to Claude Code in 19 Minutes (No Code)
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial promises to get complete beginners up and running with Claude Code in under 20 minutes, with no prior coding experience required. It's a great starting point if you've just heard about Claude Code and want a fast, practical introduction without getting bogged down in setup complexity. Watch this before diving into longer full-course videos.

highlights.title: Comparing 32 AI Coding Tools: Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, Copilot, Windsurf and More (Chinese)
highlights.summary: This Bilibili video offers a hands-on comparison of 32 AI coding tools including Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Codex, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, Kiro, and Zed, helping you quickly identify which tool fits your workflow. The author provides real-world test results rather than spec-sheet comparisons, making it actionable for developers trying to choose between tools. Even if you're committed to Claude Code, understanding the competitive landscape helps you know when to reach for a different tool.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube video aims to take developers from familiar-but-basic Claude Code usage to a more confident, productive workflow in just 30 minutes. It's ideal for developers who have already installed Claude Code and tried a few prompts but want to understand deeper features like context management and tool use. Think of it as the natural next step after a zero-to-hero beginner video.

tips_of_the_day: Use the new `Tool(param:value)` permission syntax (added in v2.1.178) to control which models your subagents can use — for example, add `Agent(model:opus)` to your deny list to prevent expensive Opus calls from nested agents running up your bill.

If you're on Windows and use Claude Code inside WSL2, update to v2.1.179 right away — the mouse-wheel scrolling bug that broke in v2.1.172 is now fixed, and partial responses from dropped connections are no longer lost.

Before committing to any single AI coding tool, spend 30 minutes testing your most common real-world task (e.g. refactoring a function, writing a test) in two or three competitors — hands-on benchmarks on your own codebase beat any YouTube comparison video.
